mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.5 percent within a two-month window, it confirms labor market recovery, which increases household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in commercial and industrial credit availability and housing starts indicates broad-based aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 5% and HOUST rises > 3% in 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-recession recovery phases signals improving capacity utilization and economies of scale, which compresses unit labor costs and expands profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available data windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, which validates analyst earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns following GDP growth above 2.5% are not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with rising federal funds rates indicates that monetary tightening is failing to contain price pressures, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS changes fails to predict negative equity returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic momentum and wage stability, which supports consumer confidence and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of PAYEMS month-over-month changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns with accuracy greater than 52% over a 15-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 450,000 units during a credit tightening cycle signals severe aggregate demand contraction, which forces downward earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive or neutral forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.
